claim: Progressive disclosure—revealing capabilities as agents need them—is more context-efficient than dumping all tools upfront like MCP servers do
actionable: Design agent skills/tools to load dynamically based on conversation state rather than loading all capabilities in the system prompt
source_title: Claude Skills Aren't Just for Claude - Here's How to Build Them for ANY Agent

claim: Skills architecture is universal: applicable to Pydantic AI, LangChain, Crew AI, or any framework—not limited to Anthropic ecosystem
actionable: Use the three-layer progressive disclosure pattern (description, main instructions, reference docs) regardless of framework or model provider
source_title: Claude Skills Aren't Just for Claude - Here's How to Build Them for ANY Agent

claim: Skill descriptions should be 50-100 words (~5% of total skill context) to avoid overwhelming the agent while remaining actionable
actionable: Keep skill.md descriptions concise; reference full instructions in separate files for progressive loading
source_title: Claude Skills Aren't Just for Claude - Here's How to Build Them for ANY Agent

claim: Main skill.md files should be 300-500 lines (~30% of total context) with optional reference documents for the third layer of progressive disclosure
actionable: Structure skills in three layers: short description (system prompt), main instructions (skill.md), and reference docs (loaded on demand)
source_title: Claude Skills Aren't Just for Claude - Here's How to Build Them for ANY Agent

claim: Dynamic system prompts injecting skill descriptions at runtime allow agents to discover capabilities as needed without overwhelming context
actionable: Parse YAML front matter from skill.md files and inject descriptions into the system prompt dynamically when agents start
source_title: Claude Skills Aren't Just for Claude - Here's How to Build Them for ANY Agent

claim: Evals prevent skills regression: test that agents use intended skills when relevant; automate with custom evaluators checking tool calls
actionable: Create YAML test cases with expected tool calls and run evaluators every time you change system prompts or add skills
source_title: Claude Skills Aren't Just for Claude - Here's How to Build Them for ANY Agent

claim: Observability (e.g., Logfire) is critical to track skill usage in production; shows token costs, traces, and where agents make decisions
actionable: Set up instrumentation to log all tool calls, inputs, and model responses; use this data to improve skills when users report issues
source_title: Claude Skills Aren't Just for Claude - Here's How to Build Them for ANY Agent

claim: Skills avoid MCP overhead by loading context only when needed; loading all tools upfront wastes tokens on tools never used in a conversation
actionable: Don't pre-load MCP servers or tools; instead, implement skills that agents request when relevant
source_title: Claude Skills Aren't Just for Claude - Here's How to Build Them for ANY Agent

claim: Tool sets in Pydantic AI (or equivalent) make skills portable; copy the tool set and reference document reading tools into your own agents
actionable: Extract load_skill and read_reference tools from template; implement two tools to read skill.md and reference docs on demand
source_title: Claude Skills Aren't Just for Claude - Here's How to Build Them for ANY Agent
